xiw: noun: A mid-frequency radio transmission or broadcast signal.
qri: noun: An active, autonomous Martas defense drone.
arta: noun: The root execution language and underlying logic of the enclave.
qax: verb: To physically strip or scavenge a hardware component.
cac: verb: To physically splice or bridge two live electrical wires.
fir: verb: To execute a continuous software process or loop.
wuw: verb: To place a system into a low-voltage idle or standby state.
vaw: verb: To aggressively overclock a hardware component.
sir: noun: A physical copper wire or data bus.
tuc: noun: A physical, hand-held maintenance tool.
cit: noun: An environmental or industrial hardware sensor.
fux: noun: Liquid coolant or industrial fluid used in thermal management.
qa: noun: A physically insulated, offline sanctuary safe from network intrusion.
cir: verb: To fetch or read raw data from a specific memory address.
qix: verb: To permanently flash or burn data onto a hardened ROM chip.
wiw: verb: To echo or output a raw signal through a hardware terminal.
qut: verb: To logically partition or reserve a sector of raw storage.
fac: verb: To securely flush a buffer or drop a software connection.
tiq: noun: A logical pointer directing execution to a specific hardware address.
sug: noun: A temporary data buffer or holding queue.
xa: noun: An empty, unwritten sector of memory; a null pointer.
I: modifier: [QUEUED] Indicates an action scheduled to happen (Future).
A: modifier: [ACTIVE] Indicates an action currently executing (Present).
U: modifier: [LOGGED] Indicates an action that has concluded and written to memory (Past).
C: modifier: [IF GATE] Evaluates the following phrase as a condition.
X: modifier: [NOT GATE] Inverts or nullifies the following opcode or register.
S: modifier: [VECTOR ABOVE] Indicates location above, on top of, or at a higher physical elevation.
M: prefix: [HOST] Binds an opcode to the speaker (I/Me), or assigns local possession (My).
N: prefix: [CLUSTER] Binds an opcode to the speaker's group (We), or assigns group possession (Our).
K: prefix: [CLIENT] Binds an opcode to the target (You), or assigns target possession (Your).
W: prefix: [EXTERNAL] Binds an opcode to a third-party, or assigns external possession.
F: modifier: [VECTOR IN / THIS] Indicates movement inside, or acts as a local memory pointer.
P: modifier: [VECTOR OUT / THAT] Indicates movement outside, or acts as an external memory pointer.
G: modifier: [VECTOR BELOW] Indicates location below, underneath, or grounded.
T: modifier: [VECTOR AT] Indicates an exact, physical connection or adjacent location.
Q: modifier: [QUERY] Casts the following phrase as a polling request for data (Question).
R: modifier: [OR GATE] Offers a diverging branch between two choices.
V: modifier: [AND GATE] Binds two conditions or opcodes together on a single logical track.
modifier: [EXECUTION TRIGGER] The Hardware Bus. Acts as a 'THEN' gate.
modifier: [SYSTEM HALT / EOF] End of File. Clears all active thread contexts.
nit: verb: To execute an optical fetch or visual ping of the immediate environment.
fasut: noun: A static liquid storage matrix, coolant vat, or uncirculated slurry basin.
tuk: modifier: A physical hardware component that is present but permanently unfunctional; a bricked or burned-out circuit.
